clusters.title: Comcast plans to split into 2 companies, spinning off NBCUniversal
clusters.neutral_headline: Comcast To Split Into Two Companies

items.title: Comcast plans to split into 2 companies, spinning off NBCUniversal
items.standfirst: Current shareholders would receive shares in both companies under the planned split, Comcast said Monday.
items.content: Current shareholders would receive shares in both companies under the planned split, Comcast said Monday.

items.title: Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into separate media business
items.standfirst: Entertainment arm to split from mobile and broadband in move that raises questions over future of Sky NewsBusiness live – latest updatesComcast is to spin off its media operation, which includes Sky and the Hollywood film studio, TV and theme park business NBCUniversal, into a separate publicly listed company.The move comes eight years after the US group, which said the separation will take a year to complete, acquired Sky’s European operations for £31bn. Continue reading...
items.content: Entertainment arm to split from mobile and broadband in move that raises questions over future of Sky NewsBusiness live – latest updatesComcast is to spin off its media operation, which includes Sky and the Hollywood film studio, TV and theme park business NBCUniversal, into a separate publicly listed company.The move comes eight years after the US group, which said the separation will take a year to complete, acquired Sky’s European operations for £31bn. Continue reading...

items.title: Comcast Shares Surge on Plan to Spin Off NBCUniversal
items.standfirst: Comcast said it plans to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into a new publicly traded company as it looks to separate its media businesses from its cable-TV and internet operations. Kelcee Griffis reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Comcast said it plans to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into a new publicly traded company as it looks to separate its media businesses from its cable-TV and internet operations. Kelcee Griffis reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky
items.standfirst: Shares rise 20% as media group outlines plan to split broadcast and studio businesses
items.content: Shares rise 20% as media group outlines plan to split broadcast and studio businesses

items.title: Comcast to Spin Off NBCUniversal and Sky, Sending Shares Soaring
items.standfirst: Comcast Corp. plans to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky, unwinding acquisitions it made more than a decade ago after struggling to demonstrate a cable and media conglomerate would benefit shareholders.
items.content: Comcast Corp. plans to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky, unwinding acquisitions it made more than a decade ago after struggling to demonstrate a cable and media conglomerate would benefit shareholders.

ai_summary: Comcast plans to split into two companies
NBCUniversal to be spun off into separate company
Split expected to take one year to complete
Current shareholders to receive shares in both companies
Move aims to separate media and cable businesses

clusters.title: Afghanistan: at least 36 killed in deadly Pakistani strikes
clusters.neutral_headline: Pakistani Airstrikes Kill Dozens In Afghanistan

items.title: Afghanistan: at least 36 killed in deadly Pakistani strikes
items.standfirst: At least 36 people were killed across three provinces of eastern #Afghanistan in Pakistani strikes. #Islamabad says it was targeting a group responsible for an attack the day before.
items.content: At least 36 people were killed across three provinces of eastern #Afghanistan in Pakistani strikes. #Islamabad says it was targeting a group responsible for an attack the day before.

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
items.standfirst: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression.
items.content: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression. (Image credit: Saifullah Zahir)

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill dozens in eastern Afghanistan
items.standfirst: Pakistan says strikes were aimed at a terrorist group while Taliban condemn ‘cowardly act of aggression’Pakistani airstrikes in three eastern provinces of Afghanistan killed 36 civilians and wounded 163 others, Afghan officials have said, as attacks between the two countries showed no sign of abating.Pakistan’s information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said the operations on Sunday night were aimed at a terrorist group his country blamed for a deadly militant attack in Karachi that killed three security personnel over the weekend. Continue reading...
items.content: Pakistan says strikes were aimed at a terrorist group while Taliban condemn ‘cowardly act of aggression’Pakistani airstrikes in three eastern provinces of Afghanistan killed 36 civilians and wounded 163 others, Afghan officials have said, as attacks between the two countries showed no sign of abating.Pakistan’s information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said the operations on Sunday night were aimed at a terrorist group his country blamed for a deadly militant attack in Karachi that killed three security personnel over the weekend. Continue reading...

items.title: Pakistan strikes eastern Afghanistan as Taliban says civilians killed
items.standfirst: Pakistan's security forces killed at least 29 militants in ground ​and air operations along the Afghanistan border, it said on Monday, while the Afghan Taliban said at least 38 civilians were killed in airstrikes. France 24's correspodent in Islamabad, Shazaib Wahlah, as the latest.
items.content: Pakistan's security forces killed at least 29 militants in ground ​and air operations along the Afghanistan border, it said on Monday, while the Afghan Taliban said at least 38 civilians were killed in airstrikes. France 24's correspodent in Islamabad, Shazaib Wahlah, as the latest.

items.title: Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan
items.standfirst: The Taliban government says civilians were killed, while Pakistan maintains they targeted militants.
items.content: The Taliban government says civilians were killed, while Pakistan maintains they targeted militants.

ai_summary: At least 36 people killed in Pakistani airstrikes
Strikes targeted militant hideouts in Afghanistan
Afghanistan condemns strikes as acts of aggression
Pakistan says it targeted terrorist group
Several others injured in the airstrikes

clusters.title: Putin admits fuel shortages after Ukrainian attacks
clusters.neutral_headline: Putin Admits Fuel Shortages After Ukrainian Attacks

items.title: Putin admits fuel shortages after Ukrainian attacks
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time Sunday that Russia is facing fuel shortages following a wave of Ukrainian attacks deep inside the country.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time Sunday that Russia is facing fuel shortages following a wave of Ukrainian attacks deep inside the country.

items.title: Ukraine strikes another Russian oil refinery as Putin admits fuel shortages
items.standfirst: Ukraine kept up its heavy drone assault on Russia, setting fire to a major oil refinery in the south, as President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time on Sunday that the country was facing a “certain deficit” of fuel and vowed to strengthen protection of oil facilities and boost fuel output.
items.content: Ukraine kept up its heavy drone assault on Russia, setting fire to a major oil refinery in the south, as President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time on Sunday that the country was facing a “certain deficit” of fuel and vowed to strengthen protection of oil facilities and boost fuel output.

items.title: Putin admits Ukrainian drone strikes are driving Russian fuel shortages
items.standfirst: President says attacks on infrastructure are causing ‘obvious’ problems but that they are ‘not critical’Vladimir Putin has admitted Russia is facing fuel shortages as Ukraine steps up its long-range drone campaign, with repeated strikes setting oil refineries ablaze and forcing multiple regions to introduce unprecedented petrol rationing.Speaking to Russian state television late on Sunday, the Russian president acknowledged for the first time that Ukrainian attacks on energy infrastructure were affecting domestic fuel supplies. “Of course, they create problems, that’s obvious,” Putin said. “Right now we’re observing a certain shortage, but it’s not critical.” Continue reading...
items.content: President says attacks on infrastructure are causing ‘obvious’ problems but that they are ‘not critical’Vladimir Putin has admitted Russia is facing fuel shortages as Ukraine steps up its long-range drone campaign, with repeated strikes setting oil refineries ablaze and forcing multiple regions to introduce unprecedented petrol rationing.Speaking to Russian state television late on Sunday, the Russian president acknowledged for the first time that Ukrainian attacks on energy infrastructure were affecting domestic fuel supplies. “Of course, they create problems, that’s obvious,” Putin said. “Right now we’re observing a certain shortage, but it’s not critical.” Continue reading...

ai_summary: Putin acknowledges fuel shortages in Russia
Shortages caused by Ukrainian drone attacks
Russia facing certain deficit of fuel
Putin vows to strengthen protection of oil facilities
Fuel output to be boosted

clusters.title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
clusters.neutral_headline: Supreme Court Strikes Down Limits On Trump

items.title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
items.standfirst: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.
items.content: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)

items.title: Supreme Court strikes down limits on Trump removing FTC members
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.
items.content: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.

ai_summary: Supreme Court strikes down 91-year-old precedent
Trump can now remove members of independent agencies
Decision gives Trump more power over agencies
Agencies previously considered independent
Supreme Court ruled in favour of Trump

items.title: British American Tobacco to slash 9,000 jobs as it turns to AI
items.standfirst: Dunhill maker to cut about a fifth of workforce, aiming to reduce costs and become more ‘technology enabled’Business live – latest updatesBritish American Tobacco (BAT) will cut about a fifth of its 47,000-strong workforce this year, as the cigarette-maker looks for ways to push down costs and become more “technology enabled”.BAT, which is one of the biggest tobacco groups in the world, has announced it will cut 5,500 jobs by the end of the year and outsource a further 3,500, affecting a ⁠total of 9,000 employees. Continue reading...

Continue reading...
